new_classes.definition_en: Liqueur (liqueur) as described in CN heading 2208, being a spirit drink to which sugar or other sweetening matter and/or flavourings have been added, classified under CN subheading 2208 70 (Chapter 22). CN 22087010 covers liqueurs in containers of 2 litres or less.

new_classes.label_en: Liqueur in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22087010)
new_classes.label_de: Likör in Behältnissen mit einem Fassungsvermögen von 2 Litern oder weniger (KN 22087010)
new_classes.definition_en: Liqueur presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN code 2208 70 10 (Chapter 22).

coverage_explanation: The legal note for 22087010 is brief and cross-references HS Explanatory Notes for heading 2208, paragraph B (liqueurs) and the third paragraph. The key distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) the product class 'Liqueur' as a subtype of Spirit, and (2) the container volume constraint (≤ 2 L) that distinguishes CN 22087010 from its sibling 22087090 (> 2 L). The detailed definitional criteria for liqueurs (addition of sugar/sweetening matter and/or flavourings to a spirit base) referenced in the HS Explanatory Notes cannot be fully formalised without access to the referenced HS notes; accordingly, coverage is set to 0.75. Disjointness from sibling spirits (Rum, Gin, Vodka, Whisky, GrapeSpirit etc.) at the Liqueur level is not axiomatised here to avoid over-commitment, but the class hierarchy already provides implicit discrimination via the named parent Spirit class.
